Composition.Programmer 1:
Fix and enhance the backend code in main.py
- Ensure Flask app correctly locates and loads all HTML templates from the 'templates' directory.
- Verify and handle the presence of required data files, especially 'data/pets.txt', ensuring it contains example pet data including pet_id=1 to prevent redirects in pet-related routes.
- Improve error handling and logging for missing data files or entries to avoid unexpected redirects.
- Maintain all original backend requirements  routing for all 10 pages, business logic for reading/writing local text files in 'data' directory, and data format compliance.
Composition.Programmer 2:
Verify and organize all frontend HTML templates in the 'templates/' directory
- Ensure all 10 HTML template files (dashboard.html, pet_listings.html, pet_details.html, add_pet.html, adoption_application.html, my_applications.html, favorites.html, messages.html, user_profile.html, admin_panel.html) are correctly saved and placed in the Flask 'templates' folder.
- Confirm that each template includes the specified elements with their IDs as per the requirements document.
- Ensure all URLs in the templates use Flask route functions (url_for) correctly.
- Coordinate with Programmer 1 to confirm template names and paths match backend expectations.
Composition.Programmer 3:
(Removed)
- The static assets (CSS, JS, images) have no reported issues and thus this subtask is removed.

Composition.Programmer 1:
Test whether the website can be accessed through local port 5000. In this test, you only need to test whether the first page of the website can be accessed through the local 5000 port.
Task 2  Test whether the first page of the website loads correctly and whether basic navigation works based on the example data provided in the Task.
Task 3  Test the elements and integrity of all pages. This includes verifying the presence and correctness of all specified elements on each page as per the requirements

background_prompt: EvoMAC is a software company powered by multiple intelligent agents, such as chief executive officer, chief human resources officer, chief product officer, chief technology officer, etc, with a multi-agent organizational structure and the mission of 'changing the digital world through programming'.
